test_cases.title: Add a single subtask to an existing task
preconditions: An existing task is present in the system
User is authenticated and has access to the task
steps: Navigate to an existing task
Initiate the add subtask action
Enter a valid title for the subtask
Submit the subtask creation form
test_cases.expected_result: The subtask is created and appears under the parent task with the provided title and a default incomplete completion status; the parent task progress indicator updates to show '0/1'.

test_cases.title: Add multiple subtasks to an existing task
preconditions: An existing task is present in the system
User is authenticated and has access to the task
steps: Navigate to an existing task
Add a first subtask with a valid title
Add a second subtask with a valid title
Add a third subtask with a valid title
test_cases.expected_result: All three subtasks appear under the parent task, each with their respective titles and incomplete status; the progress indicator displays '0/3'.

test_cases.title: Attempt to add a subtask with an empty title
preconditions: An existing task is present in the system
User is authenticated and has access to the task
steps: Navigate to an existing task
Initiate the add subtask action
Leave the title field empty
Submit the subtask creation form
test_cases.expected_result: The subtask is not created and a validation error is returned indicating that a title is required.

test_cases.title: Progress indicator updates correctly as subtasks are completed
preconditions: An existing task has three subtasks, all with incomplete status
User is authenticated and has access to the task
steps: Navigate to the parent task and note the progress indicator shows '0/3'
Mark the first subtask as complete
Observe the progress indicator on the parent task
Mark the second subtask as complete
Observe the progress indicator on the parent task
test_cases.expected_result: After marking the first subtask complete the indicator shows '1/3'; after marking the second it shows '2/3'; the parent task remains in its original incomplete state.

test_cases.title: Completing all subtasks does not automatically complete the parent task
preconditions: An existing task has two subtasks, both incomplete
The parent task has an incomplete completion status
User is authenticated and has access to the task
steps: Navigate to the parent task
Mark the first subtask as complete
Mark the second subtask as complete
Observe the progress indicator and the parent task status
test_cases.expected_result: The progress indicator shows '2/2', but the parent task status remains incomplete and is not automatically changed to complete.

test_cases.title: Unauthorized user cannot add a subtask to a task
preconditions: An existing task is present in the system
A user is authenticated but does not have write/edit permission for that task
steps: Authenticate as a user without edit permissions for the target task
Attempt to add a subtask to the task via the API or UI
test_cases.expected_result: The system rejects the request with an authorization error (e.g., HTTP 403) and no subtask is created.

assumptions: The default completion status of a newly created subtask is 'incomplete'.
Subtask titles are required and cannot be blank; no maximum length is specified so no boundary test on length is included.
The progress indicator is updated synchronously or near-synchronously upon marking a subtask complete.
The system exposes a permission model that can restrict editing tasks; at least one role exists that lacks write access to a task.

Completing a subtask that was previously complete (i.e., toggling back to incomplete) decrements the progress counter, though no explicit test is included as this behavior is not stated in the requirement.
notes: The requirement does not specify: (1) the maximum number of subtasks allowed per task — no upper boundary test is included; (2) whether subtask completion status can be toggled back to incomplete and how that affects the counter; (3) whether deleting a subtask updates the progress indicator; (4) the format of the progress indicator beyond 'completed out of total' — assumed to be a numeric fraction (e.g., '2/3'); (5) whether nested subtasks (subtasks of subtasks) are permitted — assumed not, based on the requirement only referencing a 'parent task'; (6) what roles or permission model governs subtask creation — the unauthorized test case is based on the assumption that such a model exists.
